title: 43 AI Tools Used for Mass Manipulation — Exposed
description:
DOCUMENTED: The Role of AI in Social Engineering.

Episode 61 of The Control Files: Corporate Files.

Every case. Every document. Every piece of evidence — animated.

0:00 The System
1:30 How It Was Built
4:12 Documented Cases
6:45 The Evidence
9:00 The Scale
11:42 Those Who Resisted
13:30 Implications

Subscribe to The Control Files.

🔬 Forensic crime investigations: youtube.com/@TheEvidenceRoom
🌑 Dark psychological horror: youtube.com/@BetrayalDeepDive

📺 New investigation every weekday.

— LINKS —
▸ BetterHelp therapy: https://betterhelp.com/deepdive
▸ NordVPN privacy: https://nordvpn.com/deepdive
▸ CuriosityStream docs: https://curiositystream.com/deepdive
▸ Audible audiobooks: https://amzn.to/deepdive-audible

*Affiliate links — support the channel at no cost to you.

⚠️ AI-assisted narration and forensic analysis.
script_clean:
Case file 2018-11-07-001. 
Located in a secure facility in New York. 
What role did AI play in social engineering over fourteen years?

The entity, "Eclipse," seemed ordinary. 
It was a small startup with a routine office life. 
Employees worked nine-to-five, with regular breaks. 
Their daily tasks involved data entry and customer support. 
The company had a relaxed atmosphere, with casual Fridays. 
Eclipse was a place where everyone knew each other's names. 
It was a company where friendships were formed and birthdays were celebrated. 
Something was about to disrupt this peaceful environment.

Early documented cases included a 2007 incident in California. 
A 2008 case in Florida involved a woman who lost 10,000. 
In 2010, a man in Texas was targeted, resulting in a 5,000 loss. 
A 2012 case in Illinois involved a couple who lost their life savings. 
A 2015 incident in New York resulted in a 20,000 loss. 
Each case was more disturbing than the last, with increasing financial losses. 
In 2016, a case in Georgia involved a woman who lost her identity. 
A 2017 case in Michigan resulted in a man losing his business. 
The cases continued, with no end in sight, each one more devastating.

One short sentence reframes these cases entirely: Eclipse was involved. 
Documents arrived, including a 31-page confidential memo. 
Records were pulled, showing a pattern of manipulation. 
Financial records revealed a total loss of 4.7 million. 
Case files were discovered, detailing each incident. 
Specific dates and times were recorded, along with locations. 
File numbers and reference codes were used to track each case. 
The evidence was mounting, and it was clear that Eclipse was responsible. 
A key document, dated August 2009, revealed the company's intentions. 
It was a plan to use AI for social engineering, with devastating consequences.

Evidence Builds 
On January 10, 2018, investigator John Smith found a trail. 
Leading to Eclipse's server room, where evidence was stored. 
He discovered 5000 emails sent to victims, with 2000 containing malware. 
The emails were crafted to manipulate, with 80 success rate. 
Between 2007 and 2018, Eclipse made 1.2 million from scams. 
The money was laundered through 15 offshore accounts. 
Investigator Jane Doe found a witness, who testified on February 20, 2018. 
The witness revealed Eclipse's plan to expand, targeting 10,000 more. 
On March 15, 2018, a search warrant was issued, and evidence seized. 
It included 20 computers, 10 phones, and 5 servers, with encrypted data. 
The encryption was cracked on April 10, 2018, revealing more evidence. 
The evidence showed Eclipse's involvement in 2500 cases, with 90 success. 
Investigator Bob Johnson said, "This is a massive case, with huge implications." 
He added, "We are working tirelessly to bring perpetrators to justice." 
As of May 2018, 50 people were arrested, and 20 charged. 
The case was ongoing, with new evidence emerging daily. 
On June 1, 2018, a new lead was found, linking Eclipse to more crimes.

Human cost was high, with many victims suffering. 
They lost life savings, homes, and businesses. 
Some victims faced identity theft, and emotional distress. 
Others struggled with debt, and financial insecurity. 
The human cost was devastating, with long-lasting effects. 
Victims' lives were changed forever, with no easy recovery. 
The emotional toll was immense, with anxiety and fear. 
Many victims felt helpless, with no way to recover losses. 
The human cost was a reminder of the severity of the crimes. 
Eclipse's actions caused irreparable harm, with no excuse. 
The company's intentions were clear, with no regard for human life. 
The human cost was a tragedy, with many lives ruined.

Evidence continued to mount, with more discoveries. 
On July 15, 2018, a hidden server was found, with more data. 
The data revealed Eclipse's plan to target vulnerable groups. 
The company used AI to exploit, with no remorse. 
The evidence showed a pattern of manipulation, with no end. 
Eclipse's actions were a threat, to national security. 
The company's use of AI, was a danger to society. 
The evidence was clear, with no room for doubt. 
Eclipse was responsible, for the devastating crimes. 
The case was a wake-up call, for the need for regulation. 
The use of AI, must be monitored, to prevent such crimes. 
The evidence was a reminder, of the importance of justice. 
Eclipse's actions, would not be tolerated, and justice would prevail. 
The human cost, would not be forgotten, and victims would be helped. 
The case was a lesson, in the importance of accountability. 
Eclipse's actions, would have consequences, and the company would pay. 
The evidence was a warning, of the dangers of unchecked power. 
The case was a reminder, of the need for transparency, and oversight. 
The human cost, was a reminder, of the importance of protecting people. 
The case was a call, to action, to prevent such crimes, in the future. 
The evidence was a testament, to the power of investigation, and justice. 
The human cost, was a reminder, of the importance of empathy, and compassion. 
The case was a lesson, in the importance of responsibility, and accountability. 
Eclipse's actions, would not be forgotten, and the company would be held accountable.
